title: Shared cross-intent tone axis — shared token palette + contract vs flattened value vocabulary
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1427 (a #1337 spinoff): #1337 seated an open-numbered tone dimension on Action with an action-scoped core of neutral|danger and explicitly DEFERRED the broader question — should a shared cross-intent tone vocabulary exist (one semantic-tone axis consumed by badges, alerts/banners, status indicators AND action) vs per-intent tone dimensions that diverge? Precedent: #1318/#1324 rejected a shared Emphasis Intent ('vocabularies diverge per intent -> don't flatten'). WE context: #1337 ratified relocating destructive off level onto an open-numbered tone dimension on Action (core neutral|danger), refused to bless success|warning|info ON action ('a green Confirm is unusual; danger is the one consequence tone that clearly applies to a button'), and noted the residual that ACTION KEEPS neutral|danger REGARDLESS of #1427 — so #1427 cannot widen action's blessed set, only decide whether a shared contract/palette exists above the per-intent enums. #1318/#1324 rejected one shared value LIST, choosing a per-intent variant dimension: 'What is shared is the CONTRACT, not an intent … sharing happens at the META-SCHEMA layer, never at the VALUE layer' — it did NOT reject sharing TOKENS, only flattening the value vocabulary. Current tone vocabularies in the tree (the divergence is the evidence): Action tone neutral|danger (per #1337, build pending); Status Indicator tone neutral|info|progress|positive|caution|critical; Tag tone neutral|info|positive|caution|critical|categorical; Message (alert/banner) tone neutral|positive|caution|negative; Feedback (toast) severity info|success|warning|error; System Notification severity info|success|warning|error — three different dimension NAMES (tone/severity/level) for one concept, four different value SETS across six intents, and even the intents whose prose claims a 'unified severity vocabulary' disagree (Feedback info|success|warning|error != Message neutral|positive|caution|negative), so the 'unified' claim is aspirational not realized; status-indicator's progress and tag's categorical exist only because those intents need them (strongest in-tree 'blessed subset diverges' evidence). Prior-art findings: (load-bearing) shared palette, per-component blessed subset — Adobe Spectrum ships one semantic palette (negative/notice/positive/informative) but Badge and StatusLight bless DIFFERENT subsets; Chakra splits the layers literally (status = blessed semantic subset info/warning/success/error vs colorScheme = the full ~20-color palette; Alert takes status, Button takes colorScheme + a danger scheme, buttons get no 'success status'); Ant Design = one palette, three vocabularies per component (Alert type success/info/warning/error; Badge status success/processing/default/error/warning; Button = a danger boolean — mirroring #1337's action core); Bootstrap is the one system that FLATTENS (eight contextual classes uniformly on .alert-*/.badge-*/.btn-*) and btn-warning/btn-info are widely cited as semantically odd (uniform-flattening is possible but a known smell); Radix Colors = pure token layer + thin semantic aliasing (red->danger, green->success), deliberately not dictating which components expose which semantic (cleanest proof the token layer is separable). Cross-cutting pattern: every mature system separates a shared color/token palette from a per-component value enum; the only flattener (Bootstrap) is a smell; buttons converge on danger-only, alerts/toasts carry the full set, badges/status sit between — the blessed subset provably diverges per component even when the palette is shared. Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~85%) a near-false-dichotomy resolved by SEPARATION — the shared VALUE ENUM branch (one flat neutral|danger|success|warning|info every intent consumes) is BROKEN (in-tree six intents/four value sets with progress/categorical/danger-only members meaningless elsewhere; #1337 freezes action to neutral|danger; #1318 'sharing happens at the meta-schema layer never the value layer'; Bootstrap is the lone flattener and a smell); default = per-intent blessed value enum + a SHARED tone CONTRACT (meta-schema) + a SHARED tone TOKEN palette in webtheme (WE codifies one tone meta-contract: a tone value names a semantic color/severity family the theme resolves never a hex, open-numbered per #1318; one token palette --tone-danger/--tone-success/...; each intent blesses its own subset) — the #1318 statute applied a second time (variant -> tone), DRY where real (palette + contract), divergent where real (the enums); the fully-independent status quo loses the token-layer DRY win; HIGH-LEVERAGE fork, flag for the skeptic pass (reading 'shared axis' as the flat enum is the trap #1318 sprang). (Fork 2, ~65%) reconcile the divergent dimension NAMES (tone/severity/level) + synonym sets — default: standardize the name tone + a canonical synonym table (danger=negative=critical=error; success=positive; warning=caution) so the theme resolves any synonym to one token (tone already won the #1337 naming fork); #1427 rules the canonical names and FILES the per-intent rename sweep as a separately-prioritized build (mirrors #1337 migration handling). Supported by default: open-numbered per #1318; tone is UX-only (the theme resolves the hex); action keeps neutral|danger regardless (#1337 residual, non-negotiable); resolution explicit (+) ambient project default (+) default, most-permissive unspecified -> neutral. Seams (three separable layers — the separation IS the decision): (1) the shared tone TOKEN palette in webtheme (--tone-{danger,success,...}, light/dark-aware); (2) the shared tone META-CONTRACT as a platform-decisions statute (the #1318 statute, second application); (3) the per-intent blessed VALUE enum, intent-owned and divergent — sharing never reaches this layer (#1318 holding). Report: reports/2026-06-21-cross-intent-tone-vocabulary.md.
